title: Better With Beta
description: A re-imagining of modern Minecraft with gameplay and visuals inspired by pre-b1.8.
body:
Modern Minecraft redesigned with the aim of capturing the magic of the Beta experience, both visually and in gameplay. _Heavily inspired by [Better Than Adventure](https://www.betterthanadventure.net/)._

The key design philosophy was to **create cohesion between features** by aggressively tweaking, removing, and occasionally adding blocks/items/features. The _advancement guide aims to walk the player through each and every aspect of gameplay_, particularly important given the **major changes to progression.** This modpack is certainly **more difficult than vanilla**, but is designed to _make every step forward in your journey feel like a leap_, and emphasize the parts of Minecraft that are the most fun.

![A typical landscape in the modpack.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/25dda86d936a0a6bac83bf7bc0c1ec07fd38ea25.jpeg)

**Key Features:**
- **Custom resourcepack in the style of Programmer Art!**
  - An upgraded classic feel with **1000+ custom textures.**
- **Redesigned progression!**
  - Full advancement guide, explaining nearly every block, item, and feature in the game.
  - **800+ custom recipes.**
- **Tools and items rebalanced to give everything its own function:**
  - Mine with wooden tools to get copper, then smelt it in a kiln.
  - Make Weak torches, campfires, and lanterns with coal for an early light source.
  - Leather armor now lasts significantly longer, and boots grant you feather falling.
  - Chainmail armor innately provides thorns.
  - Mine with Copper Tools to get iron, then smelt it in a furnace crafted with deepslate.
  - Iron Tools have innate fortune, while armor has blast protection.
  - Gold Tools have innate silk touch, while armor has fire protection.
  - Diamond Tools are the fastest in the game.
  - Steel Tools, acquired in The Nether from Steel Ore, have innate fortune but last longer than iron.
  - Mine Blazing Netherrack for Blaze Powder which can be crafted into a block as fuel or used to craft improved torches, campfires, and lanterns.
  - Get Crystal Armor by defeating the End Dragon.
- **Reworked workstations!:**
  - Kiln: Smelt copper, glass, hardened clay, and crack bricks.
  - Oven: Cook food. It's what it's for, after all.
  - Furnace: Smelt ores and smooth block variants.
  - Crucible: Extract minerals from blocks and items, and char blocks.
  - Crusher: Crush cobblestone to gravel to sand, bones into meal, and flowers into dye.
  - Armor Bench: Upgrade leather armor into higher tiers with special materials.
- **Classic+ hunger system!**
  - Heal directly, over time.
  - Reworked hunger items, incentivising crafting foods together to get the best results.
- **Better storage:**
  - Bundles!
  - Barrels have double capacity!
  - Trunks can be crafted from Zombie Piglin leather for portable chests.
- **Truly consistent, expanded, and diverse block palettes:**
  - A _regular and/or cobbled, polished, brick, cracked brick, mossy brick, chiseled, and pillar_ **variant** for _stone, granite, slate, limestone, deepslate, clay, sandstone, red sandstone, basalt, quartz, netherrack, charred netherrack, and black netherrack._
  - A slab, vertical slab, stairs, and wall for every regular, cobbled, polished, brick, and mossy brick variant.
- **Wood you believe it?!**
  - Wood planks replaced with colored variants for every color, which can be inter-dyed.
  - Swamp (Green), Azalea (Purple), and Hemlock (Blue) trees offer new opportunities, each with a full wood pallette.
  - Apple trees!
  - Directional wooden planks.
  - Seamless stripped logs.
  - Wooden walls for every tree type.
  - Giant mushrooms get a completely new look, with the nether variants (Nether and Glowing mushrooms) better matching with their overworld counterparts.
- **Get around differently:**
  - Sprinting is removed to incentivise thinking about movement and boost other methods.
  - Paths and other similar blocks give a massive speed boost if you construct roads.
  - Rafts are cheap, but boats require iron, and only boats can carry chests.
  - Minecart networks are cheaper, and now have self-powered rails.
  - Pigs can move faster with a significantly expanded carrot-on-a-stick durability and armor.
  - The Nether is 1:1, can only be accessed via ruined portals, and ice boats are removed.
- **Removed enchantment and experience system.**
  - Regular tools and armor now last significantly longer.
  - Innate enchantments for almost every tool and armor.


###################################################################################

**Important Notes**
- This pack is only available in English (_EN_US_). It does not add any custom items, it instead uses datapacks, custom textures, and custom display names to modify vanilla content.
- Similarly, any resource packs on top of this will almost certainly lead to inconsistencies.
- Due to the extensive nature of the changes and early stage of this pack, there are probably a lot of issues & bugs. If you find any, please email me at contact@simoneisdocha.ie.

**Credits**
> This pack wouldn't exist without the work of the [Better Than Adventure](https://www.betterthanadventure.net/) team, but also big props to them for letting me use a few of their textures. Additionally, a few of my custom textures were based on both [Programmer Art Continuation Project](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/pacp) and [Programmer Art Ultimate](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/programmer-art-ultimate), both of which are included in this pack. The mod [Maple Tree](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/mapletree), which is released under MIT License, is also included in this pack, although all of the textures are replaced.
